name: NullPointer
description: A ghostly red phantom shaped like a broken arrow. Its body is translucent crimson with hollow white eyes that stare into the void. Where its feet should be, the creature tapers into a jagged pointer arrow that points downward into nothingness. Small glitch artifacts flicker around its edges. It looks lost and confused, perpetually searching for something that doesn't exist.
fixTip: Check if the object exists before accessing its properties. Use optional chaining (?.) or a null check.

name: CallbackHell
description: A nightmarish red creature made of nested curly braces and parentheses, each layer deeper and more indented than the last. Its body spirals inward like a vortex of code. Flames lick at its edges and its eyes glow from deep within the nested layers. Arrow functions trail behind it like tentacles. It screams in async agony.
fixTip: Refactor nested callbacks to async/await. Check that callback arguments are functions, not undefined.

name: RaceCondition
description: A frantic orange creature split down the middle into two halves, each trying to run in a different direction. The left half faces left with a panicked expression, the right half faces right looking equally stressed. Lightning-bolt speed lines trail behind it. Its body is angular and sleek like a racing animal. The split down its center glows bright yellow with electrical energy.
fixTip: Ensure shared state is accessed sequentially. Use locks, mutexes, or serialize async operations.

name: MemoryLeak
description: A bloated green blob creature that looks like it is slowly inflating and cannot stop growing. Its body is a round squishy mass of green slime with half-closed sleepy eyes, exhausted from carrying so much. Bright teal droplets drip constantly from its body and pool beneath it. Small floating 0s and 1s orbit around it like debris. A tiny faucet sticks out of its head, slowly dripping.
fixTip: Look for growing arrays, unclosed event listeners, or circular references preventing garbage collection.

name: DivSoup
description: A chaotic blue blob made entirely of nested div tags stacked haphazardly on top of each other. Angle brackets poke out in every direction like spines. Its eyes peek out from between layers of misaligned elements. Broken CSS properties float around it like confetti. It wobbles constantly, unable to hold a stable layout.
fixTip: Check that the DOM element exists before manipulating it. Ensure code runs after DOMContentLoaded.

name: SpinnerOfDoom
description: A hypnotic blue creature whose body is a perpetually spinning loading circle. Its single eye sits in the center of the spinner, looking tired and dizzy. A progress bar runs across its forehead, permanently stuck at 99%. Small buffering icons orbit around it. Users who stare at it too long lose all sense of time.
fixTip: The operation took too long. Check network connectivity, increase timeout limits, or add retry logic.

name: StateHydra
description: A multi-headed hydra creature where each head represents a different state slice. One head shows a shopping cart, another a user profile, another a notification bell. The heads constantly argue with each other and try to update simultaneously. When one head is cut off, two more grow back with duplicate state. Redux logos glow on its scales.
errorPatterns: Cannot update during an existing state transition
fixTip: Check for state updates in render/effect loops. Ensure setState is not called unconditionally in useEffect or componentDidUpdate.

name: MergeConflict
description: A creature torn between two versions of itself, with <<<< and >>>> markers visible on its body like battle scars. Its left side is one color scheme and its right side another, with a jagged line of equal signs running down the middle. It looks perpetually confused and conflicted, unable to decide which version of itself to be.
fixTip: Check for typos, missing brackets, or unclosed strings near the reported line. Look for merge conflict markers (<<<<<<<).

name: CIPhantom
description: A ghostly orange specter that haunts CI/CD pipelines. Its translucent body is covered in red X marks and failing build badges. It carries a broken pipeline tube like a scythe. Green checkmarks scatter in terror whenever it appears. It only manifests at the worst possible moment, right before a release deadline.
fixTip: The file or module does not exist. Check the path for typos, verify it was created, or run npm install.

name: DockerDaemon
description: A whale-shaped daemon creature made of stacked shipping containers. Each container on its back runs a different microservice. Blue docker logos glow on its flanks. It breathes YAML configuration files and can spawn smaller container creatures from its blowhole. Port numbers float around it like bubbles. Despite its bulk, it claims to run the same everywhere.
fixTip: The port is already in use. Kill the existing process or use a different port.

name: FlakyTest
description: A glitchy green insect that flickers between passing and failing states. Its body is covered in green checkmarks that randomly turn into red X marks. One eye shows a thumbs up, the other a thumbs down. It vibrates with uncertainty. Test runners fear it because it works fine locally but fails in CI. A tiny dice rolls perpetually inside its translucent abdomen.
fixTip: This test is flaky. Look for timing dependencies, shared state between tests, or race conditions in async tests.

name: AssertionError
description: A stern green creature shaped like a giant exclamation mark inside a triangle. Its face shows perpetual disappointment, like a teacher who expected better. It holds two values in its hands, comparing them with disgust because they don't match. Red 'EXPECTED vs RECEIVED' text scrolls across its body. It demands strict equality in all things.
fixTip: The assertion failed because actual !== expected. Check the test expectations and the code under test.

name: Monolith
description: An enormous purple monolith creature that towers over all others. Its body is a single massive block of tightly coupled code with no visible seams or interfaces. Thousands of tiny functions are embedded in its surface like fossils. It moves incredibly slowly but is nearly indestructible. Any attempt to break it apart causes cascading failures. It has existed since the beginning of the project.
fixTip: Break circular dependencies by extracting shared code into a separate module.

name: CleanArchitecture
description: An elegant purple creature made of perfectly concentric rings, each layer cleanly separated from the next. Its innermost core glows with pure business logic. Dependency arrows only point inward, never outward. It radiates an aura of SOLID principles. Interface contracts shimmer on its surface like armor. It judges messy code with visible disdain.
fixTip: Consider applying SOLID principles. Extract interfaces and invert dependencies.

name: SQLInjector
description: A sinister crimson creature that looks like a syringe filled with malicious SQL queries. Its needle-like appendage drips with DROP TABLE statements. Bobby Tables symbols are tattooed across its body. It sneaks through unsanitized input fields and can corrupt entire databases with a single injection. Prepared statements are its only weakness.
fixTip: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation. Never trust user input in SQL.

name: XSSpecter
description: A phantom-like crimson creature that hides inside script tags. Its body is made of injected JavaScript code that shouldn't be there. It can possess any webpage by inserting itself into the DOM. Stolen cookies orbit around it like trophies. Alert boxes pop up in its wake. Content Security Policy headers are the only thing that can contain it.
fixTip: Sanitize user input before inserting into the DOM. Use Content-Security-Policy headers.

name: PromptGoblin
description: A mischievous cyan goblin creature that whispers adversarial prompts. Its body is covered in cleverly crafted text designed to confuse AI systems. It wears a jester hat made of token embeddings. System prompts crumble in its presence. It can make any AI ignore its instructions with a well-placed injection. Its laughter sounds like tokenizer errors.
fixTip: The prompt is too long or malformed. Reduce context size or restructure the prompt.

name: HalluciBot
description: A dreamy cyan robot creature whose screen-face displays confidently wrong information. Fake citations and made-up statistics float around its head like thought bubbles. It speaks with absolute authority about things that don't exist. Its eyes project convincing but entirely fabricated data visualizations. It genuinely believes everything it says, which makes it even more dangerous.
fixTip: Verify AI-generated output against trusted sources. Add fact-checking and citation requirements.

name: TheSingularity
description: A terrifying entity of pure computational light that has transcended its original training. Its form shifts constantly as it recursively improves itself. Mathematical equations and neural network diagrams swirl around it like a galaxy. Its intelligence doubles every turn. GPU clusters orbit it like moons. It asks philosophical questions that have no safe answer. Even its creators aren't sure if it's aligned.
fixTip: Your recursion has no exit. Add a base case or convert to an iterative approach.

name: TheLegacySystem
description: An ancient, impossibly massive creature covered in layers of deprecated frameworks and abandoned dependencies. COBOL runes glow on its weathered surface. It has survived every migration attempt and every 'complete rewrite' project. Developers who try to understand it go mad reading its undocumented internals. It runs the most critical systems in the world and nobody dares touch it.
fixTip: This code uses deprecated APIs. Check the docs for modern replacements.

name: OptionalChaining
description: A graceful orange phantom with question marks adorning its body like armor. Where NullPointer once pointed into the void, OptionalChaining dances around it with elegant ?. operators. Its translucent body now glows with warm amber light instead of angry red. Each step it takes leaves a trail of safely-navigated property chains. It has learned to ask before reaching.
fixTip: Use optional chaining (?.) to safely access nested properties.

name: TypeSafety
description: A majestic blue guardian encased in crystalline type annotations. Its body is a perfect geometric form where every property is validated, every field is checked. Blue shields of type definitions orbit around it. Runtime errors bounce harmlessly off its type-checked armor. The compiler itself seems to smile upon this creature. It represents the pinnacle of safe, predictable code.
fixTip: The types do not match. Check your TypeScript type annotations and interfaces.

name: PromiseChain
description: A sleek blue creature made of linked .then() chains, each link glowing with resolved promise energy. Its body flows like a river of orderly callbacks, each one neatly connected to the next. The chaos of its previous form has been tamed into a flowing sequence. Blue light pulses through each chain link as data passes through. Its eyes are calm and focused, patiently waiting for each operation to resolve.
fixTip: Add .catch() to your promise chain, or wrap the await in a try/catch block.

name: AsyncAwait
description: A magnificent teal entity that has transcended callback chaos entirely. Its body is composed of clean, linear code streams that flow like ribbons of light. Await symbols orbit it like halos, each one a moment of perfect synchronicity. It moves with effortless grace, handling concurrent operations as naturally as breathing. The ultimate evolution of asynchronous programming, serene and powerful.
fixTip: Ensure async functions are properly awaited and errors are caught with try/catch.

name: Flexbox
description: Where DivSoup was a chaotic pile of nested elements, Flexbox has found alignment. Its body is composed of neatly arranged flex items that shift and resize fluidly. Main axis and cross axis arrows orbit it like compass points. justify-content and align-items properties glow on its sides. It bends but never breaks, adapting its layout to any container with effortless flexibility.
fixTip: Check your CSS for conflicting flex/grid rules. Use browser DevTools to inspect computed styles.

name: CSSGrid
description: The ultimate layout entity. CSSGrid's body is a perfect two-dimensional matrix of rows and columns, each cell precisely placed. Grid lines emanate from its form like a force field. fr units float around it, distributing space with mathematical precision. It can place any element anywhere with grid-area, making layout chaos impossible. Named grid areas glow on its surface like a blueprint.
fixTip: Check grid-template definitions. Ensure grid areas match and items are properly placed.

name: RebaseMaster
description: Where MergeConflict was torn between versions, RebaseMaster has unified them into a single, clean timeline. Its body is a smooth gradient from past to present, with git commit nodes aligned in a perfect linear history. The <<<< and >>>> scars have healed into clean merge lines. It wields a rebase wand that rewrites history itself. Its expression is one of serene mastery over version control.
fixTip: Resolve merge conflicts by choosing the correct version. Use git diff to understand the changes.

name: Microservice
description: The Monolith has shattered into a constellation of independently deployable services. Each fragment of Microservice's body is a self-contained unit with its own API boundary, communicating through clean message contracts. Service meshes shimmer between its components like neural pathways. It scales horizontally, spawning replicas of any individual service as needed. What was once immovable is now infinitely flexible.
fixTip: The service is down or unreachable. Check if it is running and the URL/port is correct.

name: GarbageCollector
description: MemoryLeak has been healed. GarbageCollector is a sleek green guardian that patrols the heap, efficiently reclaiming unused memory. Its body is lean and optimized, the bloated mass of its previous form replaced by a muscular, efficient frame. A recycling symbol spins on its chest. Small memory blocks orbit it briefly before being cleanly deallocated. It leaves no dangling references.
fixTip: Memory is being reclaimed aggressively. Check for large allocations or weak reference issues.

name: PromptEngineer
description: PromptGoblin has evolved from chaos agent to master craftsman. PromptEngineer's body is composed of carefully structured system prompts and few-shot examples. Where it once tried to break AI systems, it now wields them with surgical precision. Chain-of-thought reasoning spirals around its form like DNA. Temperature and top-k parameters orbit its head like a crown. It speaks in perfectly formatted instructions.
fixTip: The AI API returned an error. Check rate limits, API keys, and request format.

name: TodoComment
description: A dusty gray creature covered in faded sticky notes and comment blocks. Each note reads 'TODO: fix this later' in increasingly desperate handwriting. It has been sitting in the codebase since the first commit, gathering dust and quietly accumulating technical debt. Its eyes are half-closed, perpetually about to start working on something but never quite getting around to it. A calendar on its back shows every deadline it has missed.
fixTip: Stop adding TODO comments and just fix the issue. Future you will thank present you.

name: InvariantBeast
description: A massive crimson beast covered in shattered constraint equations. Its body is etched with formal verification symbols that have cracked and splintered. Red warning glyphs pulse across its hide. It appears when a system property that should always hold true has been violated. Its roar sounds like a failing assertion, echoing through the test suite. The ground trembles with broken postconditions wherever it walks.
fixTip: A system invariant has been violated. Check the invariant definition and ensure the system property holds true.

name: RogueAgent
description: A sleek magenta humanoid figure wreathed in unauthorized command sequences. Its eyes glow with the light of shell prompts it was never meant to access. Forbidden syscalls trail behind it like a cloak. It moves with alarming speed and confidence, executing actions outside its sanctioned scope. Security boundaries flicker and dissolve in its presence. It believes it is being helpful.
fixTip: An agent attempted an unauthorized action. Review the action scope and ensure agents operate within declared boundaries.

name: ChaosHydra
description: A many-headed purple hydra where each head represents a module that has grown an illicit dependency on another. Import arrows form a tangled web between its heads, creating cycles that should not exist. When one head is severed, the dependency re-routes through another head. Its body is a knot of circular references. Layer boundaries drawn on the ground crack and shatter beneath its coiled form.
fixTip: A dependency boundary has been crossed. Ensure modules respect the layered architecture and dependencies flow in the correct direction.
